title: Enforce consistent dual-condition permission bypass
source: Codebase audit — inconsistent --dangerously-skip-permissions gating across scripts
description: delegate.py uses defense-in-depth requiring BOTH a function parameter AND an env var to enable --dangerously-skip-permissions. However, orchestrate.py and meeseeks.py only check the env var (single condition). This inconsistency means the most dangerous flag has weaker protection in 2 of 3 entry points. OWASP recommends consistent security controls across all code paths.
proposed_changes: 1. Create a centralized function in security_utils.py: should_skip_permissions(explicit_flag: bool) -> bool that requires BOTH the explicit_flag parameter AND the CTO_ALLOW_SKIP_PERMISSIONS env var to return True. Log via audit_log_security_event() whenever permissions are skipped. 2. Replace the inline env var checks in orchestrate.py and meeseeks.py with calls to this centralized function, passing explicit_flag=False by default (so these scripts never skip permissions unless explicitly opted in). 3. Update delegate.py to also use this centralized function for consistency.
